insights:
The single best deal is DeepSeek V4 Flash, which tops the composite score with an intelligence rating of 40 out of 60, a blended price of just $0.06 per million tokens, and a value score of 666.7—nearly 8% higher than the next best. It delivers 104 tokens per second and supports a 1000K context window, making it a strong all-rounder for production workloads where both quality and cost matter.

Among runners-up, MiMo-V2.5 offers similar pricing at $0.06 per million tokens and a value score of 616.7, with slightly lower intelligence at 37/60 and slower speed at 70 t/s, but adds a competitive arena code ELO of 1429. For code‑focused tasks, it is a solid alternative. Xiaomi’s MiMo-V2.5‑Pro pushes intelligence to 42/60 and code ELO to 1474, but at triple the price ($0.18 per million tokens), its value score drops to 233.3. DeepSeek V4 Pro also offers higher intelligence (44/60) at $0.18/M, with a value score of 244.4 and slower speed (61 t/s), making it a premium choice for tasks where marginal quality gains justify the extra cost. InclusionAI’s Ling-2.6-flash is extremely fast at 159 t/s and cheap at $0.06/M, but its low intelligence of 14/60 severely limits applicability for reasoning-heavy tasks.

For the most common use case—cost‑sensitive API calls on general reasoning and generation—the clear recommendation is DeepSeek V4 Flash. It balances intelligence, speed, and context length at a price point that is among the lowest, delivering the highest value score by a wide margin. Unless your application specifically demands top‑tier coding performance or slightly higher intelligence for niche tasks, this model offers the best total cost of ownership.
